title: Ancient DNA Extraction from Bones and Teeth
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"dnb1j","text":"Ancient DNA Extraction Protocol (spin column-based)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":51}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"73u4l","text":"This protocol outlines the procedure for extracting ancient DNA from bone or tooth powder using Monarch spin columns. The final DNA extract volume will be 35 µL.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fml9f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e1dif","text":"Based on: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"376en","text":"The spin column protocol is based on Dabney et al. 2013 (DOI: 10.1073/pnas.1314445110)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":62,"length":23}],"data":{}},{"key":"131d","text":"The modified binding buffer is from Allentoft et al. 2015 extraction (DOI: 10.1038/nature14507)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":75,"length":19}],"data":{}},{"key":"ahcn6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"amic2","text":"Protocol initially written up by Michael V. Westbury on 20-08-2018.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3g5p","text":"Annotations added by Andrea A. Cabrera and Alba Rey-Iglesia on 08-11-2019.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"28cmi","text":"The procotols.io version was created by Wenxi Li and Deon de Jager.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"DC6BD29DE93911EFBBEE0A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://doi.org/10.1073/pnas.1314445110"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"14E896891B864EF499FA36AAEF63584A","url":"https://doi.org/10.1038/nature14507"}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"bp2o4","text":"Ensure that ~50 mg of bone or tooth powder is prepared in advance (DO NOT treat with UV).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8rda3","text":"UV-treat aliquots (PB buffer, PE buffer, EB buffer, ddH2O) for 10 minutes before use.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":55,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5kkdj","text":"Do not UV Eppendorf Tubes/PCR TUBES","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"92vb8","text":"PE buffer: Add Molecular Grade Ethanol as indicated on the bottle before use.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"31ot","text":"Use Qiagen EB buffer directly if preferred.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cbnig","text":"Clean all surfaces with 5% bleach solution followed by 70% ethanol before and after use.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8pd8b","text":"Clean all equipment with 70% ethanol before and after use.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Modified Binding Buffer Prepare in bulk Option 1 (with 5M NaOAc) 500 mL Buffer PB Qiagen Catalog #19066 9 mL 5M Sodium acetate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) 1.25 mL 5M Sodium chloride Fisher Scientific Catalog #10609823 Adjust to pH 4-5 (~2 mL Hydrochloric acid) Option 2 (with 3M NaOAc, pH 5.2) 500 mL Buffer PB Qiagen Catalog #19066 15 mL Sodium acetate buffer solution 3M pH 5.2 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #S7899 2.5 mL 5M Sodium chloride Fisher Scientific Catalog #10609823 No pH adjustment necessary as the 3M sodium acetate is pH buffered at pH5.2. The NaOAc and NaCl can be added directly to the 500 mL of Buffer PB. Make sure to label it as Modified Buffer PB . Make individual aliquots in 50 mL Falcon tubes.

EBT Buffer *optional Appropriate for when NOT using LoBind Eppendorf tubes. Buffer EB Qiagen Catalog #19086 Tween 20 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P1379-500ml at a final concentration of 0.05% (v/v)

Digestion Buffer Choose the appropriate option based on [ProK] . Prepare fresh for each use. 0.5M EDTA Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #03690 Proteinase K, recombinant, PCR Grade Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #RPROTKSOL-RO Option 1: Use 10mg/mL ProK %s %s Option 2: Use 19mg/mL ProK %s %s
PE Buffer Remember to add Molecular Grade Ethanol to PE when opening a new bottle. See instructions onthe PE buffer bottle.
Qubit Quantification If desired, measure the DNA concentration using a Qubit. See Qubit protocol for details.
DNA Elution

Concentration of DNA with Amicon vivaspin 30kD column Amicon ® vivaspin column are also called centrifugal filter units . When centrifuging the Amicon columns, do not close the tubes too tight. MinElute spin columns can be used instead of Monarch columns, though the latter are preferable for smaller volumes, like that used in the elution step.
Digestion The microcentrifuge tubes are the LoBind tubes. Use a rotation machine to keep the samples well mixed during digestion.
Optional: Pre-digestion
EB Buffer Appropriate for when using LoBind Eppendorf tubes. Buffer EB Qiagen Catalog #19086 . Use as provided.
Incubate at 50 °C with constant rotation for 00:30:00 (use Parafilm to seal the lids).
In a 2 mL LoBind Eppendorf tube: Add 50 mg Sample to 1 mL extraction buffer . Resuspend the bone powder by vortexing .
Remove Parafilm and centrifuge for 00:02:00 at maximum speed to pellet the bone powder.
Remove the supernatant and add 1 mL fresh extraction buffer .
Resuspend the bone powder by vortexing .
Incubate at 37 °C with constant rotation for ~ 18:00:00 or overnight (use Parafilm to seal the lids).
Remove Parafilm from eppendorf and centrifuge for 00:02:00 min at maximum speed to pellet the bone powder.
Transfer the supernatant (~1 mL) to an Amicon® vivaspin 30kD column .

Centrifuge at ~4,000g until ~70 μL remains . Start with a 00:10:00 min centrifugation, then adjust in 00:05:00 min intervals as needed. If the volume does not decrease to ~70 uL after several rounds of centrifuging, then repeat steps 7.4-7.5 until all the remaining sample is used.
Mix the supernatant with 10x its volume of modified binding buffer in a Monarch column . Example: For 70 μL supernatant, add 700 μL of binding buffer. Mix by pipetting or inverting.
Centrifuge for 00:01:00 min at 8,000 RPM and discard the flowthrough . Tap the collection tube on a paper towel to get excess flowtrhough out before reassembling.
Add 650 µL PE buffer to the spin column.
Centrifuge at 5,000 RPM for 00:01:00 min and discard the flowthrough .
Repeat steps 7.6-7.7.
Centrifuge at maximum speed for 00:01:00 min to dry the spin column.
Place the column in a clean 1.5 mL LoBind Eppendorf tube.
Add 17.5 µL EB buffer (or EBT buffer if not using LoBind tubes) to the silica membrane and incubate for 00:10:00 min at room temperature.
Centrifuge at maximum speed for 00:00:30 seconds.
Repeat steps 8.1-8.2 for a total of 35 μL DNA extract. Store DNA at -20ºC.
creator: Vanssy Li
